config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat itu penting, tetapi tidak boleh langsung mengalahkan hak individu karena hak individu adalah batas agar mayoritas tidak semena-mena terhadap orang per orang. Dalam pengalaman saya, alasan demi “kepentingan umum” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kuasa atau menekan kelompok kecil. Bagi saya, yang adil adalah mencari keseimbangan, bukan menempatkan masyarakat di atas individu secara mutlak.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter, but they should not automatically override the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people emphasize social stability, public order, and collective goals, because those values are deeply familiar and often practical in real life. But I do not think “society first” should become a blank check for authorities or the majority to ignore personal rights, because once that happens, the individual is left with no real protection. The better principle is balance: society can ask individuals to accept some limits, but only within clear boundaries that protect basic rights and prevent abuse.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu kalau dimaknai secara mutlak, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk menekan orang kecil atau kelompok minoritas. Kepentingan umum memang penting, terutama untuk hal seperti kesehatan publik, keamanan, dan ketertiban, tetapi itu tidak boleh menghapus hak dasar individu tanpa batas yang jelas. Menurut saya, justru hak individu harus menjadi pagar agar negara atau mayoritas tidak bertindak sewenang-wenang. Jadi yang benar bukan masyarakat selalu di atas individu, melainkan keseimbangan yang tegas dan adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should have automatic priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand the appeal of collective order and social stability, and I do not dismiss them, but I have also seen how easily “for the public good” can become a vague excuse for ignoring ordinary people’s basic protections. When individual rights are treated as secondary by default, the burden usually falls on those with the least power, not on society as a whole. So I would support limited trade-offs in real emergencies, but only if there are clear rules, accountability, and firm boundaries that protect fundamental rights.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara otomatis atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai terlalu longgar, dan yang paling mudah dirugikan justru warga biasa atau kelompok minoritas yang suaranya lemah. Memang ada situasi tertentu seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan yang menuntut pembatasan, tetapi pembatasan itu harus jelas, proporsional, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Jadi bagi saya, hak individu bukan lawan kepentingan masyarakat, melainkan pagar supaya kepentingan bersama tidak berubah jadi pembenaran untuk kesewenang-wenangan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically come before the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people value order, stability, and collective responsibility, but I have also seen how easily “for the common good” can be used to silence ordinary people or weaken basic protections. When rights are treated as secondary by default, the people who suffer first are usually those with the least power, not society as a whole. Limited restrictions can be justified in real emergencies, but only with clear rules, accountability, and strong boundaries that protect fundamental rights.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
